question: Q6
title: Retrieval not strictly deterministic: residual wall-clock ranking inputs
summary: Tie-breaks are deterministic (ANN id, RRF hash/id, Stage2), but scores carry wall-clock inputs: vector useDecay=true uses julianday(now), trigger timestampBoost uses Date.now, Stage2 recency uses Date.now. So fixed query is not strictly reproducible. Large benchmark swings are INFERRED mostly model-refined queries; clock inputs are a real residual. Fix for reproducibility: inject fixed now, useDecay=false, add id tie-break to trigger channel.
